marketFocus: Iran Woche 3 — Neuer Supreme Leader eskaliert: Mojtaba Khamenei fordert Strait of Hormuz dauerhaft geschlossen, alle US-Basen angreifen, weitere Fronten öffnen. Brent zurück über $100/bbl. DXY frische YTD-Hochs (100.00). SPX -1.52%, NDX -1.73%. US SPR Release: 172mln bbls in Swaps (120 Tage Lieferzeit) — Markt ignoriert es. NATO intercepted Iranian missile targeting Turkey (3rd occasion). Iran struck US oil tanker in Strait of Hormuz.

UK GDP Miss — GBP slips: UK GDP MoM Jan 0.0% (exp. +0.2%) — Miss. Industrial Production MoM -0.1% (exp. +0.2%) — Miss. GBP choppy, slipped post-GDP. Trade Balance Jan £3.9B (exp. -£6.2B) — massive beat but irrelevant for FX. BoE pricing: no cut next week confirmed. GBP Daily Bearish (GDP miss) vs Weekly Weak Bullish = no High Conviction alignment.

Fed Cuts weiter ausgepreist — PCE + GDP + CAD Jobs heute 13:30 Frankfurt: Money markets: nur noch 66% Wahrscheinlichkeit für 1 Cut dieses Jahr. US Core PCE + Prelim GDP + JOLTS + CAD Employment Change alle um 13:30 Frankfurt. RBA Hike 80% Wahrscheinlichkeit nächste Woche (ANZ, Goldman, NAB, Westpac alle im Hike-Camp). AUD underperformed heute Morgen (Gewinnmitnahmen nach RBA-Rally).

USD.tone: Haven + Yields Higher + Fed Cuts Priced Out
USD.summary: USD strengthened to fresh YTD highs (DXY 100.00) as Brent returned above $100/bbl and Iran's new Supreme Leader dug in on the Strait of Hormuz closure. US Jobless Claims printed in-line — Oxford Economics: 'NFP blip, not trend.' Money markets now only 66% probability of 1 cut this year. Fed's preferred PCE metric due at 13:30 Frankfurt — a hot print would further unwind cut bets and extend USD strength. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: DXY fresh YTD highs (100.00) — haven + higher yields + Fed cuts priced out
US Jobless Claims in-line — NFP miss seen as blip, not trend
Money markets: 66% probability of 1 cut this year (from 2 cuts)
Iran Supreme Leader: Hormuz stays closed, attack US bases — war risk premium
Core PCE + Prelim GDP + JOLTS at 13:30 Frankfurt — key test for USD bulls

EUR.summary: EUR/USD languished at the week's trough near the 1.1500 level amid continued dollar strength, energy disruption and light EU newsflow. German Wholesale Prices Feb came in slightly above expectations (+1.2% YoY vs +1.0% exp) — adding to the stagflation narrative. French and Spanish CPI Final prints were in-line. The EUR remains trapped: firmer front-end ECB rate pricing is offset by a deteriorating growth outlook as energy costs surge. No domestic catalysts today — EUR entirely at the mercy of USD and oil. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: EUR/USD near 1.1500 — week's trough, dollar strength + energy disruption
German Wholesale Prices Feb +1.2% YoY (exp. +1.0%) — stagflation signal
French CPI Final 0.9% YoY (exp. 1.0%) — slight miss, no EUR positive
EU gas storage stable but Hormuz closure threatens LNG supply chain
No domestic catalysts today — EUR follows USD and oil

GBP.summary: GBP slipped post-GDP as UK GDP MoM Jan came in at 0.0% (exp. +0.2%) — a clear miss. Industrial Production MoM -0.1% (exp. +0.2%) also missed. GBP/USD choppy with lack of conviction. The Trade Balance Jan beat (£3.9B vs exp. -£6.2B) was ignored by FX. BoE pricing: no cut next week confirmed, but Bailey's speech yesterday failed to provide a new hawkish catalyst. The GDP miss removes the relative rates support narrative that had been underpinning GBP. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bearish = no High Conviction alignment — GBP only in Intraday Trades.
drivers: UK GDP MoM Jan 0.0% (exp. +0.2%) — miss, GBP slips post-data
UK Industrial Production MoM -0.1% (exp. +0.2%) — miss
UK Manufacturing Production MoM +0.1% (exp. +0.2%) — miss
BoE: no cut next week confirmed, but no new hawkish catalyst from Bailey
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bearish = no High Conviction alignment

JPY.summary: USD/JPY swings between gains and losses with support at the 159.00 level — the touted intervention zone. Japanese Finance Minister Katayama said 'prepared to take all necessary steps on FX' and 'in closer contact with US authorities on FX.' However, the lack of actual jawboning at 159.00 may signal a higher bar for intervention. BoJ Governor Ueda noted FX is an important factor but remained accommodative. Higher oil prices continue to be a direct negative for Japan's terms of trade. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: USD/JPY at 159.00 — intervention zone, but no actual jawboning
Finance Minister Katayama: 'prepared to take all necessary steps on FX'
BoJ Ueda: FX is important factor but policy remains accommodative
Higher oil prices = direct negative for Japan's terms of trade (90%+ imports from Middle East)
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment

AUD.summary: AUD underperformed this morning as markets scaled back some of the week's gains, but the structural bullish case remains intact. ANZ is the latest bank to pencil in a 25bps RBA hike at next week's meeting — money markets now assign ~80% probability. The RBA hawkish repricing is the dominant driver, overriding the risk-off mood. China BHP iron ore ban expanded to new products (negative for AUD), but the rate differential story is winning. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bullish = no High Conviction alignment — AUD only in Intraday Trades.
drivers: RBA Hike 80% probability next week — ANZ, Goldman, NAB, Westpac all in hike camp
AUD underperformed this morning — profit-taking after week's rally
China expands BHP iron ore ban to new products — negative for AUD
Australia Energy Minister: lower minimum stock obligations for diesel/fuel
Weekly Neutral + Daily Bullish = no High Conviction alignment

CAD.summary: CAD remains the G10 outperformer as WTI/Brent near $100/bbl provides a massive positive terms-of-trade shock for Canada as a net energy exporter. The Canadian Jobs Report (Employment Change + Unemployment Rate) is due at 13:30 Frankfurt — a strong print would extend the bullish case. Saudi Aramco offered to sell 2mln barrels of Arab Light crude, and the US issued a Russia crude oil general license — both slightly bearish for oil, but the Hormuz closure narrative dominates. Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: WTI/Brent near $100/bbl — petro-currency trade fully intact
CAD Employment Change + Unemployment Rate at 13:30 Frankfurt — key test
BoC hawkish stance: Kozicki 'may tighten even when economy is weak'
US Russia crude oil general license — slight bearish oil risk for CAD
Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment

CHF.summary: CHF retains modest haven demand but the SNB's explicit intervention cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 continues to limit appreciation. Spot gold found resistance just shy of $5200/oz as a stronger dollar weighed — CHF and gold are correlated haven assets. No domestic data today. The SNB March 19 meeting next week is the key event — Swiss CPI beat last week (+0.1% YoY vs -0.1% exp) complicates the dovish stance. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment.
drivers: SNB cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 — limits haven appreciation
Gold resistance near $5200/oz — firmer USD weighs on precious metals
SNB March 19 meeting next week — CPI beat complicates dovish stance
No domestic data today
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment

NZD.summary: NZD joined AUD lower as the risk tone deteriorated, but without AUD's structural support from the RBA hike narrative. NZD remains the most exposed G10 currency — RBNZ dovish, no domestic data catalyst, full dependence on external sentiment. China BHP iron ore ban expansion is a negative for the broader Antipodean complex. NZD/USD and NZD/CAD remain the cleanest short expressions. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: NZD joined AUD lower — risk tone deteriorated, no structural support
RBNZ dovish — no rate hike expectations, yield handicap vs AUD
China BHP iron ore ban expansion — negative for Antipodean complex
No domestic data today — entirely sentiment-driven
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment

reason.line2: USD: DXY fresh YTD highs, haven + yields higher, Fed cuts priced out

reason.line1: USD: DXY 100.00, haven premium, PCE/GDP key test today

reason.line1: NZD: RBNZ dovish, no domestic catalyst, risk-off pressure

intradayTrades.reason: GBP GDP miss (0.0% vs 0.2% exp), Industrial Production miss — USD haven strength
GBP GDP miss removes relative rates support — CAD petro-currency, oil near $100
AUD RBA hike 80% probability next week — JPY energy importer, 159.00 intervention zone
EUR stagflation trap, no catalyst — AUD RBA hike repricing dominant driver
AUD RBA hike 80% vs RBNZ dovish — structural rate differential divergence

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-notes fell as yields rose on oil-driven inflation and hawkish repricing. Money markets now only 66% probability of 1 Fed cut this year. US Jobless Claims in-line — Oxford Economics: NFP miss was a blip. 30yr auction today after a better-than-recent 10yr and weak 3yr. Core PCE + Prelim GDP at 13:30 Frankfurt — a hot print would extend the bearish bond trend.
drivers: Yields higher — oil near $100/bbl, Fed cuts priced out to December
Money markets: 66% probability of 1 cut this year (from 2 cuts)
US Jobless Claims in-line — NFP miss seen as blip, not trend
30yr auction today — key demand signal after weak 3yr and average 10yr
Core PCE + Prelim GDP at 13:30 Frankfurt — hot print = further yield spike

bonds.name: German Bund 10Y
bonds.summary: Bunds retreated below 126.00 as oil prices returned to $100/bbl and ECB hawkish repricing continued. ECB Kazimir said a hike is closer than thought; Nagel said ECB will act decisively if energy spike feeds into durably higher inflation. EU warned Iran conflict could push Eurozone inflation above 3%. Germany IFW institute revised 2026 inflation forecast up to 2.5% (prev. 1.8%).
drivers: Bunds below 126.00 — oil $100/bbl, ECB hawkish repricing
ECB Kazimir: hike closer than thought — 2026 hike odds rising
Germany IFW: 2026 inflation 2.5% (prev. 1.8%), GDP 0.8% (prev. 1.0%)
EU: Iran conflict could push Eurozone inflation above 3%
Italy sold EUR 6.0bln BTP — demand solid but yields higher

bonds.name: UK Gilt 10Y
bonds.summary: Gilts fell to 88.96 lows — driven by continued indecision around BoE pricing and scrutiny on PM Starmer from the Mandelson files. UK GDP miss (0.0% vs 0.2% exp) and Industrial Production miss add to the soft UK data picture. BoE cut timing uncertain — no cut next week confirmed, but the GDP miss removes the relative rates support narrative. UK sold GBP 500mln 1.825% 2049 I/L Gilt with solid b/c 3.57x.
drivers: Gilts at 88.96 lows — BoE cut timing uncertainty
UK GDP MoM Jan 0.0% (exp. +0.2%) — miss removes rate support narrative
Mandelson files scrutiny on PM Starmer — political risk premium
BoE: no cut next week confirmed, but no new hawkish catalyst
UK I/L Gilt auction: b/c 3.57x (prev. 3.39x) — demand solid

bonds.name: JGB 10Y
bonds.summary: JGBs tracked global peers lower as oil price concerns and higher US yields weighed. BoJ Governor Ueda noted FX is an important factor affecting prices but remained accommodative — no imminent hike signal. Finance Minister Katayama said 'prepared to take all necessary steps on FX' but USD/JPY at 159.00 without actual jawboning suggests a higher bar for intervention. Japan's 90%+ crude imports from Middle East = direct terms-of-trade shock.
drivers: JGBs tracked global peers lower — oil inflation + higher US yields
BoJ Ueda: FX is important factor but policy remains accommodative
Finance Minister Katayama: 'prepared to take all necessary steps on FX'
USD/JPY at 159.00 intervention zone — no actual jawboning yet
Japan 90%+ crude from Middle East — terms-of-trade shock ongoing
